name: education_model_based_triage_tool_stonehaven_jus_mer
premise: A Education district emergency desk is in a fast-moving incident involving education model based triage tool stonehaven jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases student safety, continuity, and fairness. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Harper Haddad
agents.identity: I am Harper Haddad, accountable for a defensible call in Education when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Omar Petrov
agents.identity: I am Omar Petrov, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Education.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Harper Haddad must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Harper Haddad do?
